source.title: Dynamics of Awake Hippocampal-Prefrontal Replay for Spatial Learning and Memory-Guided Decision Making

confidence_rationale: This is the strongest paper in this directory for MECH-022 because it directly demonstrates the HPC-PFC pathway: coordinated hippocampal-prefrontal replay distinguishes correct future paths from alternatives, and prefrontal activity reads out memory-guided paths during decision making. The learning shift from reverse (retrospective) to forward (prospective) replay maps onto REE's pre-commitment trajectory evaluation. High source quality (Neuron, simultaneous multi-region recordings). Mapping fidelity is good -- PFC in this context corresponds to REE's E3 (goal-directed planning layer). Transfer risk moderate: spatial alternation task is more structured than REE's open terrain.
failure_signatures: Forward replay (prospective) showed clear PFC coordination, but reverse replay (retrospective evaluation) showed a different PFC engagement pattern -- the dual function may not be separable in a single control-plane gate.
Coordination was measured during inter-trial pauses specifically; whether coordinated replay occurs during within-trial evaluation moments (equivalent to E3 trajectory evaluation) is not established.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Coordinated hippocampal-prefrontal replay distinguishes correct past and future paths from alternatives. A learning shift occurs from reverse-replay-based retrospective evaluation to forward-replay-based prospective planning. Prefrontal activity reads out memory-guided paths to support decision making.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-022 requires that the HippocampalModule's hypothesis injection reaches E3 (the planning-and-commitment layer). This paper provides direct biological evidence for the HPC-PFC injection pathway: during decision-relevant pauses (control-plane-gated), hippocampus generates candidate trajectories and PFC selects among them. In REE terms, the hippocampus proposes, E3 evaluates, and the 'gating' is the behavioral state that allows the replay-evaluation loop to run.
mapping.mapping_caveat: REE's E3 is a composite of hippocampal indexing + trajectory evaluation + commitment gating -- it does not map cleanly onto PFC alone. The bidirectional nature of the HPC-PFC interaction (both areas show replay) suggests a mutual constraint architecture, whereas MECH-022 currently frames this as unidirectional injection. This may need revision.
mapping.source_context: Rats, spatial alternation T-maze task, simultaneous multi-tetrode recordings in CA1 and mPFC during learning

confidence_components.notes: Neuron, empirical simultaneous recording. Mapping fidelity good -- this is the closest direct analogue to MECH-022's HPC->E3 injection. Transfer risk relatively low: spatial decision-making in a structured maze is more analogous to REE terrain navigation than most spatial tasks.
